rationale: Both candidates correctly validate the core fix (codes.audio convention, backward compat) matching the reviewer's approval rationale, but neither precisely reproduces either non-blocking GT comment (doc/module-comment mismatch; confirm legacy fallback still has a live producer). X's containment-fallback critique touches the same underlying risk as GT's doc-mismatch point but proposes a different fix; Y's voxtral_tts.py:214 comment sits in the same code area as GT's legacy-precedence point but concludes 'safe' rather than raising GT's live-producer question. Y edges out on precision/actionability with more grounded, evidence-cited findings (a genuine dead-code catch, a concrete test-gap suggestion, precise line-level fixes) versus X's shorter, valid but less specific pair of findings.
